title: The Gainax PC games are they translated by any chance
display_title: The Gainax PC games are they translated by any chance
wikitext:
[]: The Gainax PC games, are they translated by any chance?             Quote Post by toReason » Wed Aug 23, 2006 2:14 am Such as the Ayanami and Ikari Raising Projects etc.. I failed with the forums search tool :(

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Aug 23, 2006 11:20 am There's Princess Maker 2, which isn't Eva but Gainax nonetheless. It's an old strange Japanese game, but it's very entertaining. From what I gathered, most of it was OFFICIALLY translated, but they dumped it anyway, because it carried the Japanese trademark sexism, and wasn't fit for the western market. But the beta's spread out over the net, so you can get your hands on it if you search. It's worth it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Aug 23, 2006 12:16 pm No Evangelion game, regardless of platform, has been officially translated. As far as I'm aware, the only game to be fan translated is Girlfriend of Steel.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 23, 2006 12:27 pm translating and subbing an encoded video/audio stream = easy translating and subbing a video game without source code = non-trivial The best you can do is check gamefaqs for translations for menus and some of the game dialogs.

[]:             Quote Post by toReason » Thu Aug 24, 2006 2:05 am Nice one chaps *opens new tab* :) Think I'll have a look into that Princess Maker first then maybe an import 'station at some point in the future.

From what I gathered, most of it was OFFICIALLY translated, but they dumped it anyway, because it carried the Japanese trademark sexism, and wasn't fit for the western market. But the beta's spread out over the net, so you can get your hands on it if you search. It's worth it.
